projectSetup.confirmDiscardManualEdits: Switching modes will discard manual edits. Continue?
projectSetup.liturgicalTextPlaceholder: Type or paste the Latin liturgical text here...
projectSetup.manualEditsWarning: Changing the text above discards manual syllabification edits
projectSetup.migration.dropped: • Dropped boxes (words with a different count): {{count}}
projectSetup.migration.footDropped: The dropped boxes must be redrawn in the crop editor. Continue?
projectSetup.migration.head: Switching to "{{mode}}" mode changes the syllable division. The app can remap the crop boxes automatically:
projectSetup.migration.kept: • Kept boxes (words with the same count): {{count}}
projectSetup.migration.noChangedWords: No word changes division — all boxes will be preserved.
projectSetup.modeTooltip.liturgicalTypog: Original gregorio-project/hyphen-la patterns, unchanged. Preserves etymological divisions (om-ní-pot-ens, ad-o-rá-mus, quon-i-am). Use for compliance with printed liturgical typography.
projectSetup.modeTooltip.manual: Fully manual syllabification via typed hyphens.
projectSetup.modeTooltip.sung: gregorio-project/hyphen-la patterns with phonetic post-processing to match the sung convention (AISCGre Brasil / Clayton Dias / Solesmes in chant books).
projectSetup.openRecentError: Could not open {{filePath}}. The file may have been moved or deleted.
projectSetup.syllabificationHint: Edit the hyphens to adjust syllable division. Words separated by spaces, syllables by hyphens.
sectionPanel.endIndexMustBeAfterStart: The end index must be greater than or equal to the start index.

sectionPanel.title: Piece sections

tutorial.exportDocx.body1: Generates a landscape .docx file with embedded images, ready for review or publication in Word.
tutorial.exportDocx.body2: All set! You can revisit this tutorial at any time from the Help > Tutorial menu (future versions).
tutorial.exportDocx.title: 5. Export DOCX
tutorial.sliceEditor.body1: Click a syllable at the top and drag on the image to outline the box for that neume. Tab advances to the next syllable.
tutorial.sliceEditor.body2: Each box has 8 handles (like in Word). Use "Same size as 1st" to speed things up. Delete removes the active box.
tutorial.sliceEditor.title: 3. Crop Editor
tutorial.sourcesImages.body1: Add manuscripts as rows in the table. Fill in siglum, city, century, folio — or import from Gueranger in batch.
tutorial.sourcesImages.body2: In each row, Ctrl+V pastes the manuscript image from the clipboard.
tutorial.sourcesImages.title: 2. Sources & Images
tutorial.tablePreview.body1: See the finished table with all crops. Hover over a cell to zoom in. Click opens a menu to edit or remove.
tutorial.tablePreview.title: 4. Comparative Table
tutorial.textProject.body1: Type the Latin liturgical text. The automatic syllabification appears just below — you can edit the hyphens directly to adjust it.
tutorial.textProject.body2: When you create the project, you choose where to save it. Ctrl+S saves afterward.
tutorial.textProject.title: 1. Text & Project
tutorial.welcome.body1: This tool helps you build comparative neume tables from Gregorian chant manuscripts, replacing the manual process in Word.
tutorial.welcome.body2: The workflow has 5 steps. We'll walk through them quickly.
tutorial.welcome.title: Welcome to Mocquereau!
tutorial.welcome.warningBody: The software is provided "as is", without warranties. The authors are not responsible for data loss, crashes, or other defects. Back up your projects regularly.
tutorial.welcome.warningTitle: ⚠ ALPHA version — use at your own risk.
